repo_analysis.config_system: sentence-transformers library API (Python kwargs, not CLI argparse)

eval_constraints.num_examples: full (1379 pairs for STSb test; non-generative exception applies — per-example cost is sub-second)

faithful.description: Full reproduction of Table 1 SBERT-NLI-base row: evaluate on all 7 STS datasets (STS12-16, STSb, SICK-R) with cosine similarity
faithful.eval_command: python3 papers/1908.10084/run_eval.py --model sentence-transformers/bert-base-nli-mean-tokens --datasets STS12 STS13 STS14 STS15 STS16 STSb SICK-R --batch_size 16
faithful.notes: All 7 datasets are small (few hundred to ~1500 pairs each). Total ~8000 pairs. Fully feasible on CPU.

scaled.description: Evaluate SBERT-NLI-base on STSb test set only (1,379 pairs) — the primary benchmark dataset

scaling_cuts_applied.rationale: STSb is the most widely-used STS benchmark and the paper reports per-dataset scores — STSb alone is sufficient to verify the checkpoint produces correct embeddings
claims_preserved: SBERT-NLI-base achieves 77.03 Spearman on STSb test
claims_compromised: Average across all 7 STS datasets (74.89) cannot be computed from STSb alone

smoke_config.eval_command: python3 -c "from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer; m = SentenceTransformer('sentence-transformers/bert-base-nli-mean-tokens'); e = m.encode(['hello world', 'test sentence']); print('Embedding shape:', e.shape); print('SMOKE TEST PASSED')"
smoke_config.success_criteria: exit code 0 and 'Embedding shape: (2, 768)' printed to stdout

metrics_to_capture.name: spearman_cosine_stsb
metrics_to_capture.description: Spearman correlation (cosine similarity) on STSb test set

metrics_to_capture.name: pearson_cosine_stsb
metrics_to_capture.description: Pearson correlation (cosine similarity) on STSb test set

expected_output_patterns.pattern: EmbeddingSimilarityEvaluator: Evaluating the model on the

warnings: The checkpoint 'sentence-transformers/bert-base-nli-mean-tokens' is a legacy model from the original SBERT paper. The HuggingFace model hub page may show deprecation notices pointing to newer models, but this is the exact checkpoint needed for reproduction.

STS12-16 datasets require separate download via the 'mteb' or custom loading — the sentence-transformers/stsb dataset on HuggingFace only covers STSbenchmark. The eval script must handle loading each dataset appropriately.
The paper reports Spearman correlation x100 (e.g., 77.03). The library outputs raw correlation (e.g., 0.7703). The eval script should handle this conversion for comparison.
